exercises.title: Thermal State Entropy

exercises.starterCode:

    """Analyze entropy vs temperature."""
    pass
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def vn_entropy(rho):
    eig = np.linalg.eigvalsh(rho)
    eig = eig[eig > 1e-15]
    return -np.sum(eig * np.log2(eig)) if len(eig) > 0 else 0

def thermal_state(H, T):
    if T == 0:
        # Ground state
        eig, vec = np.linalg.eigh(H)
        ground = vec[:, 0]
        return np.outer(ground, ground.conj())
    beta = 1 / T
    exp_H = np.linalg.eigh(H)
    rho = np.zeros_like(H, dtype=complex)
    for i, e in enumerate(exp_H[0]):
        v = exp_H[1][:, i]
        rho += np.exp(-beta * e) * np.outer(v, v.conj())
    rho /= np.trace(rho)
    return rho

# Two-level system
H = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]])  # Energy gap = 1
Ts = np.logspace(-1, 1, 20)
entropies = [vn_entropy(thermal_state(H, T)) for T in Ts]

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
plt.semilogx(Ts, entropies)
plt.xlabel('Temperature')
plt.ylabel('Entropy (bits)')
plt.title('Thermal State Entropy')
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
hints: High T: maximally mixed, Low T: ground state
